opp.ais_why:
• On the October 8 2025 demo, Erik Palkhiwala and Yana Levin raised LLM integration preferences directly. They asked Zycus to confirm that Merlin negotiation agents would work with Stemline's corporate Google Gemini instance rather than Azure or OpenAI. The Zycus team logged an action item to confirm whether all Merlin agents support alternative corporate LLMs and report back. That is buyer-driven AI architecture.
• Stakeholders pulled for AI-driven workflow automation to compress contract development, review and approval timelines, including draft-versus-template exception reporting and collaborative intake orchestration. Liz Colucci flagged interest in AI-powered Instareview that reduces contract analysis from weeks to seconds. ERP obligations integration and clause-level risk scoring are required, not optional.
• Three calls were recorded but only the October 8 demo is in completed state with 2395 seconds of audio. The remaining sessions were not recorded. Decision sequencing follows three steps - vendor research, business case, then summer/fall budget cycle - so current timeline may slip the 2025 window. The AI engagement is concrete enough to hold Hungry at the lower bound.

champion.narrative: Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing) is the identified champion. She was the primary contact on the 2025-10-08 discovery call and was named as the Evaluator contact role in Salesforce. She has direct access to the economic buyer (they were both on the discovery call) and was actively engaged in shaping the demo requirements (requested CLM focus, DocuSign integration, biotech/pharma references). However, her engagement has been passive since the 2025-10-27 demo (8 months ago). The rep reached out to her on 2026-07-01 (today) to get feedback on the 'project mentioned at DPW NY,' but no reply has been received yet. Her current status is passive/on hold: she has not proactively pushed the evaluation forward, requested pricing, or provided post-demo feedback. The risk is that competing priorities, unresolved demo blockers (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references), or budget uncertainty have stalled her advocacy. Reactivating Liz and confirming the status of the two demo commitments is the immediate priority.

competition.narrative: Stemline is evaluating Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, and Ironclad alongside Zycus (per SF Competitors__c field). No competitive intelligence from calls, tasks, or next steps has been captured to indicate which competitor is leading, whether Stemline has demoed with any of them, or how Zycus compares. The economic buyer's request on the 2025-10-08 call for biotech/pharma references with successful CLM implementations for indirect services workflows suggests that domain fit and peer validation are the key wedges to differentiate Zycus from the competitors. The AI/Merlin positioning is also a potential wedge: the economic buyer asked whether all Merlin negotiation agents function with the relevant stakeholder (Stemline is a Google house, not Microsoft/Azure), indicating that AI capability and LLM flexibility are evaluation criteria. The wedge is: (1) Merlin AI negotiation agents with the relevant stakeholder integration for indirect services procurement (pharma-specific use case), (2) referenceable biotech/pharma customers demonstrating CLM success for indirect services workflows. However, both wedges are at risk because the two demo commitments (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references) remain unresolved 8 months post-demo. If Zycus cannot confirm Google Gemini compatibility or provide biotech references, the deal is exposed to competitors who can.

decision_process.narrative: The economic buyer outlined a 3-step decision process on the 2025-10-08 discovery call: (1) gather information (discovery and demo), (2) obtain pricing and assemble a business case, (3) seek budget approval. He stated that Stemline missed the 2025 budget cycle (evaluation began Sept/Oct 2025; annual budgeting is summer/fall) and timing for 2026 budget approval is 'TBD.' The deal is currently stalled at step 1; no step-2 activity (pricing request, business case assembly) has been logged in the 8 months since the demo. The approval chain involves a multi-person evaluation committee: procurement/sourcing (Liz, Yana), IT/AI (Vinita), legal (Anna, Jessica), quality/GCP (Michelle), finance (Ugur), and R&D clinical (Drew). The economic buyer mentioned in June 2026 (per Next Step) that the 'project back on table,' but no detail on budget approval window or business case progress has been provided. The process is on hold pending either budget clarity or resolution of the two demo commitments (Google Gemini confirmation, biotech references).

These are the confirmed evaluation criteria for the demo. The champion (Liz Colucci) and the economic buyer also requested referenceable biotech/pharma customers with successful CLM implementations for indirect services procurement workflows, indicating that peer validation and domain fit (biotech/pharma, indirect services) are decision gates. No formal RFP, scorecard, or structured use-case validation has been logged. The 2025-10-27 demo call has no post-demo feedback captured in Salesforce or Avoma, so it is unknown whether the demo met the criteria or surfaced gaps. The buyer's 3-step process (info gathering, pricing/business case, budget approval) implies that demo fit and references are prerequisites for step 2, but no step-2 activity has occurred. The evaluation criteria are partially documented; what remains unknown is whether the demo satisfied them and what additional technical or business requirements must be met to progress.

deltas.subject: Re-engage Economic Buyer (Erik Palkhiwala) directly to confirm project status and address two outstanding blockers from Oct 2025: (1) Google Gemini LLM integration confirmation (Stemline is a Google house; Erik asked explicitly on 2025-10-08 whether all Merlin agents function with the relevant stakeholder; Mike committed to confirm but no follow-up captured—this is a technical blocker), (2) biotech/pharma customer references with indirect services workflow validation (Yana and Erik requested on 2025-10-08; Justin committed to provide but no delivery confirmed). deltas.to: Run competitive review and position Zycus's AI-first wedge vs the four named competitors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, Ironclad). Stemline ran a confidential comp
deltas.subject: Run competitive review and position Zycus's AI-first wedge vs the four named competitors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, Ironclad). Stemline ran a confidential competitive evaluation; no post-demo debrief or shortlist update has been captured, so Zycus's ranking (finalist, eliminated, sole vendor) is unknown. The specific AI capabilities Erik requested on 2025-10-08—intake orchestration, clause-level exception reporting, contract-vs-template comparison to accelerate legal review, ERP obligations integration—are Merlin's technical differentiators and were the focus of the 2025-10-27 demo. Surface the post-demo technical validation (if favorable) and contrast Zycus's AI maturity (Merlin launched 2018) vs Coupa/Ariba's newer/less-mature AI, and Zycus's modular CLM-to-S2P path vs iCertis/Ironclad's CLM-only offering (Erik and Yana expressed interest in end-to-end sourcing for the future).

deltas.subject: Multi-thread to Yana Levin (Head of R&D Procurement) and re-secure Liz Colucci's active advocacy. Liz (developing champion, Evaluator contact role) drove the evaluation through Oct 2025 but has been passive/on hold since (last activity 2026-05-07, 50 days ago). Yana expressed strong interest in the end-to-end sourcing module on 2025-10-08 ('I would love to see that... for the future') and is a key stakeholder for the future-state S2P vision. Multi-threading to Yana and Liz (in parallel to Erik re-engagement) creates redundancy if Erik remains unresponsive, and re-secures the champion layer. Use the biotech/pharma references and Google Gemini confirmation as the hook to restart the conversation, then co-author a mutual action plan to move from step 1 (info gathering, completed Oct 2025) to step 2 (pricing/business case, overdue ~8 months).
